goal: Make RTX LIDAR /scan bit-reproducible across CI replays so AMCL jitter stays under 5 cm.
thoughts: Honest answer: RTX LIDAR is NOT bit-exact deterministic across drivers — denoiser + RT sampling introduce variance. Levers: (1) fix physics dt + substeps, (2) disable denoiser, (3) pin sample count per beam, (4) lock omni.replicator seed. If CI needs absolute determinism, switch to analytic lidar (isaacsim.sensors.rtx analytic model). Cite that driver-version parity is required.
code:
# 1. Inspect current LIDAR config — denoiser on?
inspect_camera(prim_path="<LIDAR_PRIM_PATH>")

# 2. Pin physics and sim clocks
set_physics_scene_config(
    scene_path="/physicsScene",
    time_steps_per_second=<60>,
    substeps=<1>,
    solver_type="TGS"
)
enable_deterministic_mode(seed=<CI_SEED>)

# 3. Disable RTX denoiser + pin samples per beam on the sensor
run_usd_script(
    description="Pin RTX LIDAR sample count and disable denoiser",
    code="""
from pxr import Usd
stage = omni.usd.get_context().get_stage()
p = stage.GetPrimAtPath('<LIDAR_PRIM_PATH>')
p.GetAttribute('omni:sensor:Core:denoiser:enabled').Set(False)
p.GetAttribute('omni:sensor:Core:samplesPerBeam').Set(<N_SAMPLES>)
p.GetAttribute('omni:sensor:Core:seed').Set(<CI_SEED>)
"""
)

# 4. If tolerance still missed: switch profile to analytic LIDAR
lookup_knowledge(query="isaacsim analytic lidar model deterministic replacement for RTX")

# Caveat: cross-driver bit-exact is NOT promised by NVIDIA.
failure_modes: Claiming RTX LIDAR can be bit-exact across GPU/driver versions (it cannot)
Pinning only physics seed — RT path still jitters
Dropping denoiser without warning — point variance goes UP
Analytic-lidar swap ignored — Dimitri loses another week of CI flakes
